title: Required escrow title is silently discarded

evidence.quote: const [form, setForm] = useState({ title: "", beneficiary: "", amount: "" });
<Field label={t("titleLabel")} value={form.title} onChange={v => setForm(f => ({ ...f, title: v }))} placeholder={t("titlePlaceholder")} />
args: [form.beneficiary as `0x${string}`, resolvedArbiter, descriptions, amounts, 0, useAIArbiter, referrer],

reasoning: The page requires users to enter a title and stores it in form.title, but neither escrow creation branch includes that value in the contract call or persists it elsewhere in the owned files. A user can successfully create an escrow after entering a title, but that title is lost immediately after submission.
recommendation: Either remove the title field if escrows do not support titles, or persist it through the intended storage path before/after the transaction and associate it with the created escrow address.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests were provided for this feature, and there is no assertion that title input affects the submitted escrow payload or stored escrow metadata.
suggestedRegressionTest: Submit both simple and milestone create forms with a non-empty title and assert the title is persisted or deliberately absent from the UI contract if titles are unsupported.
minimumFixScope: Update the create submit flow and any expected persistence/API integration for escrow metadata, or remove the required title field and translations from this page.
